commit.message:
fix(favicon): Base64 data URI方式に変更 - 全ブラウザ対応 (v0.44.4)

## 問題
- href='/favicon-dev.svg?v1' → URLクエリ付きでも表示されない
- data:URIのSVG内<>が未エンコードでHTML属性を破壊

## 解決
- base64エンコードdata URI方式に変更
  data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2Zy...
- サーバー配信不要、ブラウザキャッシュ問題を完全回避
- 全ブラウザ (Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge) で確実に動作

## 内容
- DEV: オレンジ(#F97316)背景 + 白歯車アイコン (16x16 viewBox)
- PROD: ブルー(#1D4ED8)背景 + 白サーバーアイコン (16x16 viewBox)
- dev/ 55ページ・prod/ 46ページ全て更新
- favicon-dev.svg / favicon-prod.svg もシンプル版に更新

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(gateway): ICE dual-mode transport — accept obfs4 + plain simultaneously

construct-config:
- Add ice_enabled (ICE_ENABLED, default false)
- Add ice_port (ICE_PORT, default 9443)
- Add ice_server_key (ICE_SERVER_KEY, base64 52-byte server identity)
- Add ice_iat_mode (ICE_IAT_MODE, 0/1/2)

gateway/Cargo.toml:
- Add construct-ice dependency (local path)

gateway/src/main.rs:
- If ICE_ENABLED=true: bind Obfs4Listener on ICE_PORT, spawn accept loop
- Each obfs4 connection: hyper_util auto-serve bridges Request<Incoming>
  to Request<axum::body::Body> and routes through the same Axum Router
- ice_load_or_generate(): loads from ICE_SERVER_KEY or generates ephemeral
  key with prominent warning to set the env var for production
- Logs bridge_line on startup so ops can distribute to censored clients

shared/tests/queue_test.rs: add missing ICE fields to Config literal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: normalise tag variants in scraper

Add TAG_ALIASES map that consolidates common variants:
- Drum & Bass / Drum and bass / Drum'n'Bass / D&B → Drum & Bass
- Soundtracks / Soundtrtack → Soundtrack
- Downtmpo / Downtemp → Downtempo
- Slow-Fi / Slo fi → Lo-fi
- Vapourware → Vaporwave
- Instumental / Intrumental hip hop → Instrumental Hip Hop
- etc.

Re-normalises existing episodes on every run so alias changes
are always applied retroactively. Deduplicates tags per episode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add ServerConfig::to_bytes / from_bytes for key persistence

Serialize server identity to 52 raw bytes (secret[32] || node_id[20]).
Base64-encode and store in ICE_SERVER_KEY env var to survive restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add comment to pages.yml upload step to trigger GitHub Pages deploy

Forces a pages.yml run to deploy the WASM build artifacts that were
committed to docs/app/ by the release workflow.

feat(fast_time): add UnwindSafe + RefUnwindSafe assertions (#129)

## Summary

Add static assertions verifying that all `fast_time` types implement
`UnwindSafe + RefUnwindSafe`. All types already satisfy the auto trait
requirements, so no explicit `impl` blocks are needed - the assertions
serve as a regression guard.

Also add guidance to `AGENTS.md` on using `--body-file` for `gh pr
create` to avoid PowerShell mangling backticks in PR descriptions.

## Types verified

**Public types:**
- `Instant`
- `Clock`

**Internal types (Windows + Linux):**
- `TimeSourceImpl`
- `BuildTargetPlatform`
- `BuildTargetBindings`
- `RustPlatform`
- `RustTimeSource`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: folo
repository.description: Mechanisms for high-performance hardware-aware programming in Rust

commit.message:
Enable parallelism again (#12747)

* why...

* add sctx.pool

* Fix pool usage, enable parallel analyzer, fix data races

- Add Parallel.run_with_pool to wrap operations in Task.run (fixes
  Unhandled(Wait) exception from missing effect handler)
- Update all call sites to use run_with_pool instead of bare Lazy.force
- Enable parallel analyzer by default (remove opt-in gate)
- Make apply_params_stack domain-local via Domain.DLS (fixes data race)
- Make alloc_mid use Atomic (consistency with alloc_var)
- Protect DCE CfMaybeUsed writes with field_marker_mutex (fixes flag race)

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move apply_params_stack to uctx

* investigate if we're actually looking at the problem

* revert

* focus

* focus more

* Replace process_stubs.c with pure OCaml implementation for OCaml 5 multi-domain safety (#12757)

* Initial plan

* Use run_in_new_pool for filter phases to avoid idle domains during eval execution

The persistent pool (via run_with_pool) kept domain workers alive during eval
interpretation and callbacks, causing subprocess/IPC failures in eval tests.
Revert to run_in_new_pool which tears down the pool after each parallel section,
matching the pattern that works on development. The sctx.pool and run_with_pool
remain available for generators (e.g. genjvm) that use them during code generation.

Also restore the EnableParallelAnalyzer guard since the analyzer was specifically
identified as leaking when run in parallel (#12414).

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert filters.ml to base branch state

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make process_stubs.c safe for OCaml 5 multi-domain programs

Two changes to fix eval subprocess failures when domainslib pool
domains are alive:

1. Add caml_enter_blocking_section/caml_leave_blocking_section to all
   blocking process operations (read, write, waitpid, WaitForSingleObject,
   ReadFile, WriteFile). Without these, the calling domain holds its
   runtime lock during blocking I/O, preventing stop-the-world GC from
   proceeding when worker domains need it. This causes GC stalls that
   lead to pipe I/O corruption and timing issues.

2. Replace fork()+exec() with posix_spawn() on Unix. OCaml 5's
   Unix.fork explicitly refuses to fork when domains are spawned
   (caml_domain_is_multicore check). process_stubs.c bypassed this
   safety check by calling fork() directly. In multi-threaded programs,
   fork() can deadlock when worker threads hold internal locks (malloc
   arenas, glibc internals). posix_spawn() avoids this by using
   clone(CLONE_VM|CLONE_VFORK) internally on Linux.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace process_stubs.c with pure OCaml implementation

Remove the C stubs entirely and implement Process using
Unix.create_process, which is domain-safe under OCaml 5.
This also refactors PipeThings.run_command to use Process.run
instead of duplicating the pipe+spawn logic.

The old C stubs had two issues in OCaml 5:
1. Missing caml_enter_blocking_section around blocking I/O
2. Direct fork() which is unsafe with multiple domains

Unix.create_process handles both correctly (it uses posix_spawn
internally on modern systems and properly releases the runtime lock).

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix error handling: convert Unix.Unix_error to Failure for eval compatibility

The old C stubs used caml_failwith for all errors, which eval catches
as Dynamic. Unix.create_process raises Unix.Unix_error instead, which
eval couldn't catch. Convert all Unix errors to Failure to preserve
backward compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* catch Unix_error in callers instead

* don't turn exit status into exceptions

* make Eof extend Exception

* catch constructor exception too

* back to running everything

* hmm

* Handle non-existent commands in Process.run without throwing

When Unix.create_process fails (e.g. command not found), return a
process with pre-closed pipes (reads return EOF) and exit code 127,
matching the old fork+exec behavior where fork always succeeded and
exec failure manifested as a non-zero exit code.

Add misc test to verify non-existent commands don't throw exceptions.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Assert output in process-nonexistent misc test, guard kill against pid=0

The test now prints stdout_empty, stderr_empty, and exit_code values
and asserts them via compile.hxml.stdout (expected: empty pipes, code 127).

Also add pid > 0 guard in Process.kill as defense-in-depth against
sending signals to process group 0 for failed process objects.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve Unix.Unix_error info in all Process operations

- run: writes error message to stderr pipe so callers can read it
- read_stdout/read_stderr/write_stdin/close_stdin: include the
  Unix error details (function, arg, message) in Failure exceptions
- Add unix_error_msg helper shared by all operations
- Test checks stderr is non-empty (not a specific message string)
  for cross-platform compatibility

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* reformat

* avoid some code duplication

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Simon Krajewski <simon@haxe.org>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

Expose joint physical properties in Blender panel

Add per-bone UI fields and fskel export for:
- compliance (XPBD softness, 0 = rigid)
- damping (viscous velocity dissipation)
- yield_strength (plastic deformation threshold)
- break_strength (joint removal threshold)

Fields appear under 'Physical Properties' in the Joint section
for any joint type except None. Values are written into joint_desc
in the fskel JSON, matching the C-side loader/writer.

feat(ui): 開発/本番環境別ファビコン追加 (v0.44.3)

## 変更内容

### ファビコン新規作成
- frontend/favicon-dev.svg: オレンジ背景 + レンチアイコン (DEV)
- frontend/favicon-prod.svg: ブルー背景 + サーバーラックアイコン (PROD)

### 全HTMLページにfaviconリンク追加
- frontend/dev/ 55ページ: /favicon-dev.svg を参照
- frontend/prod/ 46ページ: /favicon-prod.svg を参照

### API配信ルート追加 (backend/api/main.py)
- GET /favicon-dev.svg → frontend/favicon-dev.svg (image/svg+xml)
- GET /favicon-prod.svg → frontend/favicon-prod.svg (image/svg+xml)
- GET /favicon.ico → ENVに応じてdev/prodを自動選択
- /dashboard ルート: ENVに応じてdev/prodディレクトリを選択
- catch-all /{page}: ENVに応じてdev/prodディレクトリを選択（修正）

### 効果
- ブラウザタブでDEV(オレンジ)/PROD(ブルー)を一目で識別可能
- 誤環境での操作防止に寄与

commit.message:
feat(stats): add Image Stats panel with mean, median, std dev per channel (#14)

This pull request adds a new feature to display image statistics
alongside the histogram panel in the sidebar. The main changes include
introducing a new `ImageStatsPanel` component, implementing utility
functions and types for calculating image statistics, and updating
translation strings to support the new UI elements.

**New Image Statistics Feature:**

* Added the `ImageStatsPanel` component to the sidebar, which displays
mean, median, and standard deviation for each color channel and
luminance, using the histogram data.
(`src/features/histogram/components/ImageStatsPanel.tsx`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7b35dddbb7e9cd4afd847b2f3eef23cc3ec87358530a24ab19386f16bc97291eR1-R78)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R8)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R111)

**Supporting Types and Utilities:**

* Defined new types: `ChannelStats` and `ImageStats` to represent
statistical data for image channels. (`src/types/index.ts`)
* Implemented `computeChannelStats` and `computeImageStats` utility
functions to calculate statistics from histogram data.
(`src/utils/histogram.ts`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e9b7ccc919d556566ca402af5e52dcffcbfd2be1244c29bee4b4f355234d404bR26-R60)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e9b7ccc919d556566ca402af5e52dcffcbfd2be1244c29bee4b4f355234d404bL1-R1)

**Localization Updates:**

* Added translation strings for the new image statistics panel,
including labels and placeholder text.
(`src/locales/en/translation.json`)…nnel

Implements the histogram-based stats feature (R/G/B/Luminance rows ×
Mean/Median/StdDev columns) in a new sidebar panel below the Histogram.

commit.message:
Per-joint damping, yield, and break strength

Add physical properties to phys_joint_t for realistic ragdoll and
destruction behavior:

- damping: viscous coefficient dissipating relative velocity at joint
- yield_strength: impulse threshold for plastic deformation (shifts
  rest orientation toward current via nlerp blend)
- break_strength: impulse threshold for joint removal
- accumulated_impulse: tracks total impulse magnitude per joint
- broken flag: marks joints for post-tick removal

Solver integration (tick_parallel.c):
- After lambda writeback, compute per-joint impulse magnitude
- Accumulate into joint->accumulated_impulse
- When yield threshold exceeded, blend rest_relative_orient 10%
  toward current relative orientation and reset accumulator
- When break threshold exceeded, set broken=1
- Post-tick sweep removes broken joints in reverse index order

Also:
- Rename stiffness/damping → spring_stiffness/spring_damping in
  distance joint fields to avoid duplicate member names
- fskel writer emits compliance/damping/yield_strength/break_strength
- Broken joints produce 0 constraint rows (skipped by builder)
- New test file p122_joint_properties_tests.c (8 tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove [skip ci] from Wasm deploy commit so pages.yml rebuilds GitHub Pages

The WASM deploy step commits new .wasm/.js files to docs/app/ with
[skip ci], which suppresses the pages.yml workflow. Without pages.yml
running, GitHub Pages never picks up the new WASM assets. Removing
[skip ci] lets the push to docs/ trigger a Pages rebuild as intended.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Harden Terraform security, add scalability knobs, fix CI (lint + test + migrations) (#55)

* Initial plan

* Add GCP deployment infrastructure with Terraform, Kamal config, and deploy workflow

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: tighten IAM roles, configurable SSH ranges, fix outputs, add workflow permissions

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden Terraform security and add scalability for future growth

Security:
- Remove google_service_account_key (anti-pattern: keys leaked to state)
- Replace roles/compute.admin with least-privilege roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1
- Add OS Login, Shielded VM (secure boot, vTPM, integrity monitoring)
- Narrow VM SA scopes from cloud-platform to logging-write, monitoring-write, storage-ro
- Enable VPC Flow Logs on subnet
- Add firewall rule logging on all rules
- Add explicit default-deny ingress firewall rule
- Tighten internal firewall from 10.0.0.0/8 to subnet CIDR
- Enable require_ssl on Cloud SQL connections
- Enable transit encryption on Redis (SERVER_AUTHENTICATION)
- Add descriptions to all firewall rules
- Enable PostgreSQL audit logging (log_checkpoints, log_connections, log_disconnections)
- Add variable validation (project_id format, password length, DB availability, etc.)

Scalability:
- Cloud SQL: REGIONAL HA (default), disk autoresize, 50GB initial, 200 max_connections
- Cloud SQL: Optional read replicas (db_read_replica_count variable)
- Cloud SQL: Query Insights enabled, 30-day backup retention
- Redis: STANDARD_HA tier (default) with alternative zone failover
- Subnet widened from /24 (254 IPs) to /20 (4094 IPs)
- Cloud Router + Cloud NAT for outbound connectivity
- Artifact Registry cleanup policy (keep latest 10 images)
- Enable servicenetworking, logging, monitoring APIs
- Configurable deletion_protection, disk sizes, connection limits

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review feedback: align PITR retention, fix NAT output, add AR delete policy

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: squash migrations, fix RuboCop, fix Phlex autoloading, fix pre-existing test bugs

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add 8 controller specs, fix pre-existing test bugs, update SimpleCov config

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: separate list_depth from render depth, improve height estimation

- Separate list_depth from indent (render depth) in the rendering pipeline:
  lists now use list_depth for bullet style and visual indentation,
  blockquotes reset list_depth=0 for children since they handle offset
  via scope_builder. Fixes wrong bullet style (◦ instead of •) and
  16px over-indent for lists inside blockquotes.

- Height estimation improvements:
  - estimate_list_height_at_depth deducts indent_px per list nesting level
  - estimate_table_height adds 14px scrollbar height for wide tables
  - estimate_quote_height resets list_depth for inner blocks

- Add 7 integration tests locking in rendering quality:
  list_depth in blockquotes, nested structures, wide table scrollbar,
  spacing consistency, verification doc accuracy, block type coverage,
  and multi-width height accuracy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: push to branch in detached HEAD state during Wasm deploy

When release.yml checks out the repo at a tag SHA, git is in detached
HEAD state. 'git push' fails with exit 128 in that state. Use explicit
'git push origin HEAD:refs/heads/master' to push the commit regardless
of current HEAD state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: update IsOpenTelemetryRegistered for OTel SDK v1.15.0

OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting v1.15.0 renamed the hosted service
from OpenTelemetrySdkHostedService to TelemetryHostedService. The
IsOpenTelemetryRegistered guard was checking only the old name, so it
always returned false in v1.15.0, allowing UseOtlpExporter() to be
called a second time after AddServiceDefaults() had already called it.

The duplicate registration triggered OtlpExporterBuilder's new
EnsureSingleUseOtlpExporterRegistration validation on startup when
running via Aspire AppHost.

Fix: check both class names so the guard works with OTel v1.11–1.14
(OpenTelemetrySdkHostedService) and v1.15+ (TelemetryHostedService).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: ingest full git commit history from GitHub modal

Problems fixed:
1. GitHub modal was POSTing to /v1/research/github (404) — corrected
   to /v1/github/repos with proper {url, bucket} body shape.
2. Tarball-only ingestion captures a code snapshot but no history —
   searching for 'why was X changed' or 'what did we fix in March' found
   nothing because commit messages were never indexed.

Changes:
- github-ingest-service.ts: add ingestGitHistory() — pages through
  GitHub Commits API (100/page), formats each commit as:
    ## {sha} — {date}
    Author: {name}
    {message}
    Files:
      M engine/src/search.ts (+22 -4)
  Ingests the full log as compound github:{owner}/{repo}/commit-history.md
  with buckets [user-bucket, 'git-history'] so it can be filtered.

- git.ts: accept include_history boolean in POST /v1/github/repos.
  Runs syncRepo() first, then ingestGitHistory() in background.

- index.html: fix route URL; add 'Include full commit history' checkbox
  (on by default) with descriptive subtext.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(auth): cache getUser() to eliminate redundant auth network calls

Replace direct supabase.auth.getUser() calls (network round-trip) with
authService.getToken() which uses a 5-minute cache via getCurrentUser().

Before: 8-9 getUser() network calls per page load (one per API request)
After: 1 getUser() call per 5 minutes (cached), getSession() for token

Files changed:
- auth-service.ts: getToken() uses cached getCurrentUser() instead of getUser()
- api.ts (user-dashboard): uses authService.getToken() instead of direct calls
- query-client.ts: uses authService.getToken() instead of direct calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(systemd): venv使用でpydantic互換性修正 + WebSocketテスト更新 (v0.44.1)

## 変更内容

### Systemd修正（起動失敗を解決）
- dev/prodサービスの実行ファイルを ~/.local/bin/ から venv/bin/ に変更
- VIRTUAL_ENV環境変数を追加
- PATHをvenvのbinディレクトリ優先に変更
- gunicornをvenvにインストール (v25.1.0)
- 原因: pydantic._internal._signature 不一致エラー

### テスト更新
- test_websocket_api.py: 全25テストをfirst-message auth対応に書き換え

### ドキュメント
- CHANGELOG.md: v0.44.0エントリ追加

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address code review — fix Rust double-execution, fix test JSON, fix Pydantic deprecation

- rabbit_executor/src/main.rs: spawn with Stdio::piped() and drain stdout/stderr
  via background threads, eliminating double-execution of commands. Use the
  single 'start' timer for total duration_seconds. Fixes non-idempotent side
  effects and incorrect timing from the previous re-run approach.
- tests/test_brain.py: fix mock command string to valid JSON (avoid unescaped
  double-quotes inside JSON string literal).
- autorabbit/models.py: replace deprecated json_encoders config with Pydantic v2
  PlainSerializer annotated type for datetime ISO serialization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

MYTUBE-146 Test: Load public video watch page — Video.js player initializes and HLS stream plays (#271)

* MYTUBE-146 test: automate Test: Load public video watch page — Video.js player initializes and HLS stream plays

* Add DB presetup fixture for stable ready video in MYTUBE-146 tests

Creates testuser_mytube146 and a ready video with hls_manifest_path before
Playwright tests run. Injects MYTUBE_146_VIDEO_ID so ready_video fixture
picks it up directly. Teardown cleans up only that user's data via
_cleanup_users, leaving other DB rows intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* MYTUBE-146 test: automate Test: Load public video watch page — Video.js player initializes and HLS stream plays

* test(MYTUBE-146): fix HLS mock route and control bar visibility

- Change _HLS_TEMPLATE to HTTPS GCS URL so Playwright can intercept
  it (Mixed Content blocks HTTP subresources from HTTPS pages before
  CDP Fetch.requestPaused fires, making route() a no-op for gs:// URLs)
- Add page.route() lambda matching .m3u8 to serve a minimal valid HLS
  playlist, preventing vjs-error / vjs-controls-disabled state
- Click play in fixture to trigger vjs-has-started; app CSS requires
  this class before the control bar becomes visible (display:flex)
- Update click_play() to fall back to .vjs-play-control when the big
  play button is hidden (e.g. in vjs-ended state after empty playlist)
- Fix _VIDEO_JS_ELEMENT selector to include video.vjs-tech (Video.js v8
  restructures the DOM so the <video> receives vjs-tech not video-js)
- Add is_playing_or_ended() to accept vjs-ended for zero-duration stream

All 8 tests now pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ac): reset ac_project_state to idle on gated/failed cycle + inject INCEPTION.md into agent context

- workflows.py: after _run_workflow_background finishes with gated/failed status,
  update ac_project_state.status='idle' so watchdog doesn't retry infinitely
- engine.py: in _build_node_context, auto-inject INCEPTION.md content into
  project_context if file exists — ensures TDD agents always have spec without
  needing to call code_read first
